essence: Moves goods across long distances with precision, responsibility, and mastery of large vehicles.

identity.title: HGV Driver
identity.summary: Operates heavy goods vehicles to transport cargo safely and efficiently across regional or national routes while following strict safety and driving regulations.

salary.notes: Long‑haul, hazardous materials, tanker work, and night shifts can exceed £50k–£60k+.

qualifications: Category C or C+E HGV licence
Knowledge of road safety and tachograph regulations
Good spatial awareness and vehicle‑handling skills

lifestyle.description: HGV drivers transport goods using large vehicles across local, regional, or long‑distance routes. Days involve loading checks, route planning, long periods of focused driving, managing delivery schedules, and completing safety and compliance paperwork. Some roles require overnight stays or multi‑day trips, while others follow regular local routes. The work is independent, structured, and responsibility‑heavy. This lifestyle suits individuals who enjoy driving, routine, and working autonomously.

habits: Plans routes and rest breaks carefully
Maintains accurate delivery and tachograph records
Handles cargo with care and attention
